slides.title: The Blueprint 🏗️
slides.content:
An **Interface** allows us to name a specific object shape and reuse that shape anywhere in our code.

It acts like a custom blueprint for our data.

slides.title: Creating an Interface
slides.content: We use the `interface` keyword and typically capitalize the name.

quizData.question: What is the primary benefit of defining an `interface`?

options.text: It makes your app run faster in the browser.
It gives you a reusable name for a specific object shape, keeping code much cleaner.
It hides the object data from hackers.

quizData.explanation: Interfaces allow you to define a complex shape once, and reuse it everywhere cleanly.

slides.title: Functions & Interfaces
slides.content: You can now use your custom Interface exactly like you would use a core type like `string` or `number`.

slides.title: Compiles to Nothing 💨
slides.content: Interfaces only exist in TypeScript to help you catch errors in your editor. When compiled to JavaScript, the `interface` code completely vanishes!
